original_text:
### Tint Rules
- Campaign headline "D" and "M": **≈40% tint** of the headline color (Gold or Brand Blue colorway).
- Light Blue callout box: **70% tint**.
- Golden Sand callout box: **30% tint**.
- Wave top layers: **70% transparency**.
- No other ad-hoc tints — use palette values or these specified tints only.

relations.note: The exclusivity ban on ad-hoc tints closes the set defined by the specific per-component tint bindings.

quotes: ≈40% tint** of the headline color (Gold or Brand Blue colorway)

rules.intent: Lock the exact opacity treatment for each tinted brand element so translucency stays consistent across executions.
rules.rule_text: Tint/opacity values are locked per component: the campaign headline letters ('D' and 'M') carry ~40% tint of the headline colorway (Gold or Brand Blue), the Light Blue callout fill sits at 70% tint, the Golden Sand callout fill sits at 30% tint, and wave top layers render at 70% transparency.

effect.reserved_for: the specified tint tokens (campaign headline 40%, Light Blue callout 70%, Golden Sand callout 30%, wave top layer 70%) or full palette values
effect.subject: non-palette tint/opacity values

quotes: No other ad-hoc tints — use palette values or these specified tints only.
No other ad-hoc tints — use palette values or these specified tints only.

rules.intent: Prevent uncontrolled proliferation of transparency values that would dilute brand color consistency.
rules.rule_text: Designers must not invent ad-hoc tint/opacity levels; only the full palette colors or the specifically documented tints (40% campaign headline letters, 70% Light Blue callout, 30% Golden Sand callout, 70% wave top layer) are permitted.
